reasoning: Panel verdict split resolved under decisions/0027. Send is wrapped pre-connect (L327); onmessage is wrapped post-connect (L342-360) by capturing the client-assigned handler and delegating, so inbound handshake traffic escapes a logger whose stated intent is to log all traffic — the mismatch is exhibited. The finding's remediation (patch onmessage before connect) is wrong: the SDK client assigns transport.onmessage inside connect() and would overwrite a pre-connect patch, so the existing ordering is required. Under 0027 r3 (confirm branch): confirm with the corrected direction in suggested_fix (a transport-level tap, or documenting the handshake gap as a limitation — not reordering the patch). Test-scoped and fully masked in effect → grades info.
